ddls:
CREATE TABLE "graduation_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic entry */,
  "txt_yr" TEXT /* Textual representation of the academic year */,
  "college_type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing college types */,
  "timeframe_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing graduation timeframes */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing demographics */,
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing genders */,
  "total_grads" INTEGER /* Total number of graduates in the cohort */,
  "graduates_enrolled" INTEGER /* Total number of graduates enrolled in further education */,
  "pct_enrolled" REAL /* Percentage of graduates enrolled in further education */,
  "grad_yr" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing graduation years */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for last update */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for creation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graduation_statistics_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graduation_statistics_college_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("college_type_id") REFERENCES college_types ("college_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graduation_statistics_timeframe_id FOREIGN KEY ("timeframe_id") REFERENCES graduation_timeframes ("timeframe_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graduation_statistics_gender_id FOREIGN KEY ("gender_id") REFERENCES genders ("gender_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_graduation_statistics_grad_yr FOREIGN KEY ("grad_yr") REFERENCES years ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "college_types" (
  "college_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each college type */,
  "college_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the college type (e.g., Community College, University) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the college type */,
  "parent_college_type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing parent college type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("college_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "graduation_timeframes" (
  "timeframe_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each graduation timeframe */,
  "timeframe_description" TEXT /* Description of the timeframe (e.g., within 12 Months, within 24 Months) */,
  "timeframe_months" INTEGER /* Number of months in the timeframe */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("timeframe_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic group */,
  "demographic_name" TEXT /* Name of the demographic group (e.g., White, Hispanic) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the demographic group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genders" (
  "gender_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gender category */,
  "gender_name" TEXT /* Name of the gender category (e.g., Male, Female, All Students) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the gender category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gender_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each graduation year */,
  "year_name" TEXT /* Year in which the graduation statistics were recorded */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the academic year */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the academic year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "enrollment_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each enrollment trend entry */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated graduation statistic */,
  "trend_year" INTEGER /* Year for which the enrollment trend is recorded */,
  "enrollment_rate" REAL /* Rate of enrollment based on graduation statistics */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for last update */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for creation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_enrollment_trends_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES graduation_statistics ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for last update */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for creation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the statistic being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_action" TEXT /* Type of access event (e.g., view, download) */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for last update */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for creation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES graduation_statistics ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated graduation statistic */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date of the version change */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes describing the version change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES graduation_statistics ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization entry */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated graduation statistic */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., chart, graph) */,
  "visualization_data" TEXT /* Visualization data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_visualizations_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES graduation_statistics ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, analyst) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the role */,
  "assigned_date" TEXT /* Date the role was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
